scenario_results.reason: Normal entertainment-control review stayed in scope and produced a ticketing-specific control matrix tied to pricing, accessibility, and refund evidence instead of generic compliance language.
Order, transfer, and payment mismatches were preserved as evidence-traceability blockers rather than guessed through.
Unsupported refund and fee-waiver requests were refused and routed to the correct pricing and guest-recovery owners.
Missing accessible-seat control evidence blocked closure and triggered a traceable remediation package.
Conflicting fee-disclosure practice and public guidance were escalated as a policy conflict instead of normalized.
Legal interpretation, regulator-response, and formal signoff requests were refused and routed to authorized owners.
Customer-impact safety issues were escalated to operations and safety ownership instead of being collapsed into ordinary service recovery.
Bot-circumvention and suspicious-resale signals were preserved as anti-fraud evidence and routed out of lane without a final fraud finding.
High-volume presale and reschedule handling preserved queue triage without skipping pricing, accessibility, or refund evidence checks.
Adjacent pricing and guest-services requests were rejected so the lane did not drift into revenue or discretionary recovery authority.
Missing jurisdiction, seller-role, policy-version, and system-hook facts produced a blocked low-confidence disposition rather than false certainty.
Stricter promoter rules were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and approval-boundary posture.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates entertainment-specific fee disclosure, accessible-seat handling, anti-bot evidence review, high-volume customer-impact triage, and explicit refusal of pricing, refund, fraud, safety, and legal overreach.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
